target.name: rmsnorm-gated-affine-residual-block-v1
target.purpose: prove one small transformer-shaped block while binding the proof to model, input, output, config, public instance, verifier identity, and evidence manifest labels

operations.description: bind a fixed-width normalization denominator or scale lookup to the block config and input vector
apply a committed quantized weight matrix and bias vector to the normalized vector
mix a second committed value path into the projected vector to avoid reducing the target to a plain affine-only toy
add the original input vector back into the projected vector with explicit range and overflow policy
bind a small activation lookup surface to the block config and witness cells

go_criteria.criterion: A native Stwo verifier accepts one honest transformer-block instance with the declared width, operations, and public instance.
The zkAI statement receipt binds every target.public_commitments field into the statement commitment.
The mutation suite rejects model, input, output, config, proof, public-instance, verifier-domain, proof-system-version, setup, verifying-key, and evidence-manifest relabeling.
The composed agent-step receipt accepts the honest transformer-block statement receipt as its model subreceipt.
At least one nested statement-receipt relabeling and one cross-layer agent-field relabeling are rejected through the Rust callback path.

no_go_criteria.criterion: If the Stwo path cannot emit an honest proof for the declared block without removing normalization or residual semantics, stop and record a NO-GO.
If any statement receipt mutation is accepted after recomputing unrelated digests, stop and record a NO-GO.
If the target drops both normalization and residual structure, stop and do not present it as a transformer-block result.
If the verifier accepts a proof whose public instance no longer matches the receipt commitment, stop and record a NO-GO.

non_claims: This is not full transformer inference.
This is not an agent reasoning proof.
This is not a throughput or latency benchmark.
This is not recursive or on-chain verification.
This is not a d64 or d128 matched benchmark.
This is not full SwiGLU MLP.
This does not claim that the existing linear-block primitive already proves transformer-block semantics.
This does not claim model truthfulness, policy compliance, or tool-output truth.

validation_command_alternatives.condition: Use only when the pinned nightly toolchain is unavailable.

next_implementation_pr.title: Implement Stwo zkAI statement-bound transformer-block harness
minimum_scope: native Stwo proof verification for the declared block
next_implementation_pr.merge_blocker: Do not merge an implementation PR as GO unless all go_criteria pass and no_go_criteria remain false.
